data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <img class="Image" alt="Brian Taylor, maestro cervecero y propietario de Whiner Beer Company, se encuentra en el bar del antiguo Whiner Beer Company y el futuro SomosMonos Taproom, ubicado en The Plant en el barrio de Back of the Yards, el viernes 6 de marzo de 2026." srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/42e6194/2147483647/strip/true/crop/2500x1403+0+132/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F03%2F4f%2F893be8b44736976e13a8b6df3f4f%2Fbeercrash-260315-2.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/461f4ee/2147483647/strip/true/crop/2500x1403+0+132/resize/980x550!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F03%2F4f%2F893be8b44736976e13a8b6df3f4f%2Fbeercrash-260315-2.jpg 2x" width="490" height="275" data-src="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/42e6194/2147483647/strip/true/crop/2500x1403+0+132/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F03%2F4f%2F893be8b44736976e13a8b6df3f4f%2Fbeercrash-260315-2.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" > </picture> <div class="Figure-content"><figcaption class="Figure-caption"><p>“Definitivamente intentamos cambiar, manteniendo, obviamente, la cerveza como nuestra principal fuente de ingresos”, dijo Brian Taylor, co-propietario de Whiner Beer Co.</p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Tyler Pasciak LaRiviere/Sun-Times</p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p>La cerveza más vendida de Whiner es Le Tub, una refrescante saison añejada en barril o una cerveza al estilo de granja belga. Su última edición de primavera es una cerveza de sabor a sandía conocida como Bubble Tub.</p><p>Algunas cervecerías se inspiraron en el movimiento “California sober” y la avalancha de dispensarios de THC después de que Illinois legalizara la marihuana para consumo comercial en 2020, abriendo la puerta para bebidas infundidas con THC. Pero incluso a medida que los productos con THC derivado del cáñamo se vuelven cada vez más populares, la industria sigue siendo en gran parte no regulada.</p><p>Eso lo convierte en una opción inestable. El gobernador JB Pritzker ha amenazado con una prohibición en Illinois, y el Concejo Municipal de Chicago casi prohibió el THC derivado del cáñamo en enero. (Finalmente, la Municipalidad <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/city-hall/2026/01/21/chicago-hemp-ban-thc-beverages-topical-marty-quinn-brandon-johnson" target="_blank" >creó una excepción</a> permitiendo excepciones para bebidas y ungüentos).</p><p>Para algunos cerveceros, el riesgo es simplemente demasiado grande, dijo Gary Gulley, propietario y fundador de Alarmist.</p><p>“Esa es un área [legal] gris”, dijo. “Cada cervecería intentará hacer ese mismo producto. Entonces, volverás a estar donde empezaste, tratando de diferenciarte”.</p><p>La pandemia de COVID-19 también dejó a algunos cerveceros con una resaca financiera. Los propietarios aún tenían que pagar edificios, equipos y producción. Gulley tomó la difícil decisión de cerrar para evitar declararse en bancarrota.</p><p>“Tuvimos que cerrar el bar y si no tienes un bar, no tienes una cervecería”, dijo Gulley. “Asumí, al igual que muchas personas, que saldríamos adelante y la gente empezaría a regresar... Pero para muchos de nosotros, asumimos más deudas solo para sobrellevar el COVID”.</p><p>Los precios del grano, lúpulo y otros materiales también han aumentado a lo largo de los años: Buckman de Illuminated citó un aumento de casi el 30% interanual desde 2021. La capa adicional de aranceles del presidente Donald Trump sobre productos importados duplicó el precio del aluminio que muchas cervecerías compran para envasar sus productos.</p><p>En 2017, Whiner compró latas de una empresa canadiense a 9 centavos por lata, pero ahora el precio es de aproximadamente 16 centavos por lata.</p><p>“Todo esto sólo pone más presión sobre nosotros para aumentar precios, y eso simplemente ahuyenta a la gente. Es como una espada de doble filo”, agregó Taylor de Whiner.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure class="Figure"><a class="AnchorLink" id="image-9b701f" name="image-9b701f"></a> <picture data-crop="medium"> <source type="image/webp" width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/961535a/2147483647/strip/true/crop/2500x1403+0+132/resize/490x275!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F50%2F9e%2F8deaa36b44118546cd0c513ab257%2Fgrannyevents-062325-17.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/1029961/2147483647/strip/true/crop/2500x1403+0+132/resize/980x550!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F50%2F9e%2F8deaa36b44118546cd0c513ab257%2Fgrannyevents-062325-17.jpg 2x" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <source width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/c0266b4/2147483647/strip/true/crop/2500x1403+0+132/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F50%2F9e%2F8deaa36b44118546cd0c513ab257%2Fgrannyevents-062325-17.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <img class="Image" alt="Jessica Woodburn enseña una clase sobre cómo hacer suculentas de fieltro en Old Irving Brewing Co., en 4419 W. Montrose Ave., el martes 17 de junio de 2025." srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/c0266b4/2147483647/strip/true/crop/2500x1403+0+132/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F50%2F9e%2F8deaa36b44118546cd0c513ab257%2Fgrannyevents-062325-17.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/7d00f9c/2147483647/strip/true/crop/2500x1403+0+132/resize/980x550!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F50%2F9e%2F8deaa36b44118546cd0c513ab257%2Fgrannyevents-062325-17.jpg 2x" width="490" height="275" data-src="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/c0266b4/2147483647/strip/true/crop/2500x1403+0+132/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F50%2F9e%2F8deaa36b44118546cd0c513ab257%2Fgrannyevents-062325-17.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" > </picture> <div class="Figure-content"><figcaption class="Figure-caption"><p>Las cervecerías han diversificado sus opciones de entretenimiento en los últimos años, ampliándose más allá de las noches de trivia y conciertos. Aquí, Jessica Woodburn enseña una clase sobre cómo hacer suculentas tejidas en Old Irving Brewing Co.</p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Zubaer Khan/Sun-Times</p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p><b>Las cervecerías deben mantenerse ágiles </b></p><p>Chicago sigue siendo, y probablemente siempre será, una ciudad cervecera. Algunas de las cervecerías que sobreviven en la ciudad dicen que están intentando todo para mantenerse a flote, incluyendo subir precios.</p><p>Aumentar precios se ha vuelto inevitable, pero recortar costos no lo es, dijo Paul Kreiner, cervecero principal de Noon Whistle.</p><p>“Hemos mordido el anzuelo con algunas de estas cosas, y lamentablemente estamos haciendo márgenes más pequeños”, dijo Kreiner. “Pero tenemos que mantenernos competitivos con los grandes y también tenemos que ser fieles a nosotros mismos y asegurarnos de que estamos haciendo la mejor cerveza posible”.</p><p>Pero en una era de inflación, ¿es suficiente un aumento de precios?</p><p>Noon Whistle se mantiene, en palabras de Kreiner, “ágil” y “adelantado a los tiempos”, prestando mucha atención a tendencias como cervezas que incorporan sabores de guayaba y mango, y sirviendo comida en su bar en Lombard.</p><p>La cervecería ha hecho un esfuerzo por atraer a la audiencia con medidas llamativas, como un Malört infundido con cigarras. Bebidas curiosas y creativas atraen a la gente, o al menos, generan conversación sobre la marca.</p><p>Pero la clave del éxito es, sin duda, doble, dijo Kreiner. “Estamos tratando de adelantarnos a esas tendencias”, dijo, “pero también, ¿qué están bebiendo las personas y comprando de manera constante?”.</p><p>Para Noon Whistle, eso es la IPA (India pale ale) turbia cítrica más vendida durante todo el año conocida como <a class="Link" href="https://www.noonwhistlebrewing.com/beers/year-round-beers/" target="_blank" >Gummylicious</a>.</p><p>“Todos sabemos que la industria cervecera o de licores ha disminuido un poco”, dijo Rose-Hamblin de Old Irving. “Poder ofrecer comida y un lugar donde la gente pueda cenar y una ubicación más conveniente son cosas que realmente ayudan”.</p><p>Y la capacidad de una cervecería para crear espacios significativos para que las personas se conecten, especialmente en un mundo posterior al confinamiento por la pandemia, es lo que la gente busca cada vez más en 2026, según investigaciones de consumidores. Los clientes de Old Irving Brewing disfrutan de noches de trivia y juegos.</p><p>Y debido a que siempre habrá una rotación natural en el mercado, a medida que una cervecería cierra, otra se expande. En 2024, Whiner Beer Co. invitó a SomosMonos Cervercería a servir su producto junto al propio en un bar compartido llamado The Jungle.</p><p>Ahora, mientras Whiner se retira, SomosMonos aumentará sus operaciones en The Plant. La pareja de esposos Rocío y Víctor Santoyo, junto a su amigo de toda la vida René Lemus, asumirán el contrato de arrendamiento y todo el equipo de elaboración que conlleva.</p><p>El trío —que ha participado en festivales de cerveza y publicidad emergente durante años— ha incorporado música en sus planes para la cervecería. Debido a que Víctor y Lemus se conocieron de adolescentes y eventualmente formaron una banda, comparten el deseo de un espacio donde artistas musicales del lado sur puedan presentarse.</p><p>Ahora, están organizando eventos con actos musicales locales como <a class="Link" href="https://www.instagram.com/braidedjanes/" target="_blank" >Braided Janes</a> y <a class="Link" href="https://www.instagram.com/fuegodecumbiaband/" target="_blank" >Fuego de Cumbia</a> cada fin de semana. Su bar está abierto de jueves a domingo, pero el equipo está abierto a extender sus horarios en el futuro.</p><p>“Ahora que tenemos esta oportunidad, no podemos dejarla pasar”, dijo Lemus.</p><p><i>Traducido con una herramienta de inteligencia artificial (AI) y editado por </i><a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/la-voz" target="_blank" ><i>La Voz Chicago</i></a></p>
entries.author: Ambar Colón

entries.title: Seeking special prosecutor to target ICE, coalition says O'Neill Burke turned 'blind eye' to feds' violence
entries.summary: <p>Cook County State’s Attorney Eileen<b> </b>O’Neill<b> </b>Burke<b> </b>has turned a “blind eye”<b> </b>to “egregious acts of violence”<b> </b>by the federal agents behind last fall’s Operation Midway Blitz, a new coalition of more than 200 elected officials, clergy, journalists and attorneys argued in a court filing Thursday.</p><p>Despite demands that agents once led by U.S. Border Patrol Cmdr. Gregory Bovino <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/public-safety/2026/02/06/how-can-chicagos-top-prosecutor-hold-ice-accountable" target="_blank" >be prosecuted for state crimes</a> — like murder, assault, perjury<b> </b>or even conspiracy — O’Neill Burke has<b> </b>“never stated publicly that she has any intention to investigate these crimes,” they said.</p><p>That may be for fear of retaliation from President Donald Trump, they added.</p><p>That’s the argument made in a newly filed petition that makes the most aggressive pitch yet for a special prosecutor to investigate the agents who descended upon Chicago last fall. It was filed by the Loevy and Loevy<b> </b>law firm on behalf of a coalition that includes U.S. Reps. Jesus “Chuy” Garcia and Delia Ramirez, both Democrats, and former U.S. District Chief Judge Ruben Castillo, who was appointed to the bench in 1994 by President Bill Clinton.</p><p>It was filed Thursday in Cook County Circuit Court before<b> </b>Judge Erica<b> </b>Reddick, the presiding judge of the criminal division, ahead of a noon news conference.</p><div class="RelatedList Enhancement" data-module data-align-center> <div class="RelatedList-title">Related</div> <ul class="RelatedList-items"> <li class="RelatedList-items-item"> <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/public-safety/2026/02/06/how-can-chicagos-top-prosecutor-hold-ice-accountable" >How can Chicago’s top prosecutor hold ICE accountable?</a> </li> </ul> </div> <p>“Federal agents killed and maimed people, terrorized communities, and unleashed extreme force and chemical weapons indiscriminately against law-abiding residents who posed no threat to their safety,” the petition states,<b> </b>according to a copy obtained by the Chicago Sun-Times.</p><p>Meanwhile, “other state prosecutors in Illinois and around the country have initiated investigations into similar criminal conduct by federal agents … or announced their intention to investigate such conduct,” according to the petition.</p><p>It’s unclear whether a path to a special prosecutor exists for the coalition under the law. But O’Neill Burke says the petition will only make it more difficult for her office “to prosecute and win convictions” against agents who committed crimes during the deportation campaign.</p><p>“The stakes are too high for us to get this wrong,” O’Neill Burke, a Democrat, said in a statement earlier this week, “and I will strenuously oppose this petition.”</p><p>Still, it could increase the pressure on O’Neill Burke, even if unsuccessful. Loevy & Loevy used its lawsuit in federal court last fall to secure strategic wins, like court appearances and depositions from federal officials, as well as the public release of a tranche of records.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure class="Figure"><a class="AnchorLink" id="image-600000" name="image-600000"></a> <picture data-crop="medium"> <source type="image/webp" width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/1614a86/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6122x3436+0+324/resize/490x275!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fcb%2F19%2F537420374dd69cab66179d8b5b1d%2Fellis-260109-06.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/c466f97/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6122x3436+0+324/resize/980x550!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fcb%2F19%2F537420374dd69cab66179d8b5b1d%2Fellis-260109-06.jpg 2x" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <source width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/b4b6863/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6122x3436+0+324/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fcb%2F19%2F537420374dd69cab66179d8b5b1d%2Fellis-260109-06.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <img class="Image" alt="ELLIS-260109-06.jpg" srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/b4b6863/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6122x3436+0+324/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fcb%2F19%2F537420374dd69cab66179d8b5b1d%2Fellis-260109-06.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/2a5c027/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6122x3436+0+324/resize/980x550!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fcb%2F19%2F537420374dd69cab66179d8b5b1d%2Fellis-260109-06.jpg 2x" width="490" height="275" data-src="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/b4b6863/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6122x3436+0+324/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fcb%2F19%2F537420374dd69cab66179d8b5b1d%2Fellis-260109-06.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" > </picture> <div class="Figure-content"><figcaption class="Figure-caption"><p>Steve Art (left), attorney at Loevy & Loevy, alongside Craig Futterman, clinical professor of law at University of Chicago Law School, speaks in the lobby of the Dirksen Federal Courthouse on Jan. 8. Loevy & Loevy now represents a coalition seeking a special prosecutor to investigate and charge federal agents involved in Operation Midway Blitz.</p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Anthony Vazquez/Sun-Times</p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p>That’s even though the case was ultimately dismissed, and a federal appeals court vacated<b> </b>a sweeping preliminary injunction handed down by U.S. District Judge Sara Ellis.</p><p>Under the law, the new petition could lead to a hearing over whether O’Neill Burke has an actual conflict of interest. The petitioners also asked the judge for permission to “present a list of candidates” for special prosecutor or to “have input into the selection process.”</p><p>The coalition includes the Chicago News Guild, which represents journalists at the Chicago Sun-Times.</p><p>O’Neill Burke has publicly decried the actions of the Trump administration but maintains that her office is limited in its ability to prosecute on-duty federal agents.</p><p>She <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/immigration/2026/02/19/chicago-ice-crime-border-patrol-trump-midway-blitz-alex-pretti-renee-good-eileen-oneill-burke" target="_blank" >released her own guidance</a> for pursuing these sorts of cases last month after criticizing a plan put forth by Mayor Brandon Johnson, which her office argued raised major concerns of political interference.</p><p>Under her guidance, a law enforcement agency would lead a criminal investigation by collecting and preserving evidence. Then, prosecutors would conduct an independent review of all evidence and make charging decisions.</p><p>However, prosecuting federal agents<b> </b>in state court would require <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/immigration/2026/01/08/can-state-officials-prosecute-the-feds-calls-grow-after-minneapolis-shooting" target="_blank" >showing<b> </b>that they acted unreasonably</a> while fulfilling their duties.</p><div class="RelatedList Enhancement" data-module data-align-center> <div class="RelatedList-title">Related</div> <ul class="RelatedList-items"> <li class="RelatedList-items-item"> <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/immigration/2026/02/19/chicago-ice-crime-border-patrol-trump-midway-blitz-alex-pretti-renee-good-eileen-oneill-burke" >Cook County’s top prosecutor outlines plan to charge ICE agents with crimes</a> </li> </ul> </div> <p>As of this week, O’Neill Burke’s office said no local agencies had brought cases against any on-duty immigration officials.</p><p>Prosecutors are entrusted with discretion to choose which cases they pursue. But the coalition argued O’Neill Burke’s impartiality “has been irrevocably tainted”<b> </b>by her failure to respond to public demands to investigate Midway Blitz agents.</p><p>“The residents of Cook County, citizens of Chicago, and elected officials at local, state, and federal levels have formalized public demands for the fair, impartial, and unencumbered investigation of allegations of criminal conduct by federal agents,” the petition states. “The state’s attorney has decided not to initiate any investigation.”</p><p>The group noted that O’Neill Burke works regularly with federal law enforcement. It also pointed to the Justice Department’s investigations of Trump’s political foes, including New York Attorney General Letitia James and Fani Willis, the district attorney in Fulton County, Georgia.</p><p>“At minimum, the threat of retaliation reduces public confidence that State’s Attorney [O’Neill Burke] will exercise her discretion fairly when determining whether to investigate or bring charges,” the coalition wrote in its petition.</p><p>“To expect her to investigate and prosecute federal agents who committed crimes in her own jurisdiction is highly improbable, as evidenced by her own inaction,” it added. “An independent special prosecutor, shielded from political conflict, is therefore necessary.”</p><p>The petition prominently recounts the Oct. 4 <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/immigration/2026/02/03/marimar-martinez-shot-border-patrol-chicago-speaks-out-after-good-pretti-deaths-i-am-their-voice" target="_blank" >shooting of Marimar Martinez</a>, as well as the Sept. 12 killing<b> </b>of <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/immigration/2025/09/15/silverio-villegas-gonzalez-fatally-shot-by-ice-agents-touched-the-lives-of-so-many-loved-ones-say" target="_blank" >Silverio<b> </b>Villegas<b> </b>González</a>. In both instances, the coalition argued that agents “lied” about what happened.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure class="Figure"><a class="AnchorLink" id="image-620001" name="image-620001"></a> <picture data-crop="medium"> <source type="image/webp" width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/f884aae/2147483647/strip/true/crop/3110x1745+0+0/resize/490x275!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fb0%2F04%2F1db7d61747f79df87515c45fc1a5%2Fmartinez-260202-005.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/4968696/2147483647/strip/true/crop/3110x1745+0+0/resize/980x550!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fb0%2F04%2F1db7d61747f79df87515c45fc1a5%2Fmartinez-260202-005.jpg 2x" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <source width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/5b80f98/2147483647/strip/true/crop/3110x1745+0+0/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fb0%2F04%2F1db7d61747f79df87515c45fc1a5%2Fmartinez-260202-005.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <img class="Image" alt="MARTINEZ_260202-005.jpg" srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/5b80f98/2147483647/strip/true/crop/3110x1745+0+0/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fb0%2F04%2F1db7d61747f79df87515c45fc1a5%2Fmartinez-260202-005.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/337601b/2147483647/strip/true/crop/3110x1745+0+0/resize/980x550!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fb0%2F04%2F1db7d61747f79df87515c45fc1a5%2Fmartinez-260202-005.jpg 2x" width="490" height="275" data-src="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/5b80f98/2147483647/strip/true/crop/3110x1745+0+0/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fb0%2F04%2F1db7d61747f79df87515c45fc1a5%2Fmartinez-260202-005.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" > </picture> <div class="Figure-content"><figcaption class="Figure-caption"><p>Marimar Martinez stands in the Chicago Public Media lobby at Navy Pier on Feb. 1. Martinez survived five gunshots from a Border Patrol agent in Chicago last fall. </p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Candace Dane Chambers/Sun-Times</p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p>Christopher<b> </b>Parente, Martinez’s attorney, recently said there is an ongoing investigation<b> </b>into Border Patrol agent Charles Exum, who shot Martinez five times on the Southwest Side. The investigation is being handled by<b> </b>the U.S. attorney’s office<b> </b>in South Bend, Indiana, Parente said.</p><p>It’s unclear where that investigation stands, though. Parente told the Sun-Times, "we remain hopeful that the federal government will do its job and conduct a full and fair investigation of Marimar's shooting."</p><p>"If the federal government fails to carry out its mission as promised in Marimar's case, Cook County must be empowered to pick up the mantle of leadership and ensure justice is done for Marimar and all other victims of federal agent misconduct," Parente said.</p><p>The coalition’s petition catalogs alleged batteries, assaults, kidnappings and other violence at the hands of federal agents. It even makes a case for conspiracy charges, alleging that agents “undertook coordinated efforts over the course of hours, days, and months to commit serious crimes against the citizens and residents of Cook County.”</p><p>Though court precedent shows that a “political alliance” can create a conflict of interest, it also notes that a petitioner must plead “specific facts”<b> </b>to show a state’s attorney “would not zealously represent” the people.</p><p>A petitioner could also show<b> </b>that a state’s attorney has abandoned her duty through “an impropriety or insufficiency in the state’s attorney’s investigation,” according to past rulings.</p><div class="RelatedList Enhancement" data-module data-align-center> <div class="RelatedList-title">Related</div> <ul class="RelatedList-items"> <li class="RelatedList-items-item"> <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/immigration/2026/01/08/can-state-officials-prosecute-the-feds-calls-grow-after-minneapolis-shooting" >Can state officials prosecute the feds? Calls grow after Minneapolis shooting</a> </li> </ul> </div> <p>Special prosecutors have been used in the past in response to potential conflicts of interest or alleged misconduct within the Cook County state’s attorney’s office. Often they’re appointed to investigate and prosecute specific cases. </p><p>In 2016, <a class="Link" href="https://www.wbez.org/crime/2016/06/02/judge-to-appoint-special-prosecutor-in-laquan-mcdonald-case" target="_blank" ><u>a special prosecutor took over the murder case against Chicago Police Officer Jason Van Dyke</u></a>, who fatally shot 17-year-old Laquan McDonald. Civil rights lawyers had long questioned former Cook County State’s Attorney Anita Alvarez’s objectivity in the case and criticized her for taking more than a year to charge Van Dyke. But a special prosecutor was only appointed after Alvarez lost re-election and recused herself from the case. </p><p>In 2019, <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/2019/8/23/20828849/jussie-smollett-special-prosecutor-dan-webb" target="_blank" ><u>a special prosecutor was assigned to reopen the Jussie Smollett case</u></a> and investigate former Cook County State’s Attorney Kim Foxx’s handling of the charges. The special prosecutor secured a conviction against Smollett for lying about an alleged hate crime, but it was overturned years later.</p><p>There have also been examples of special prosecutors given much broader assignments, though. In 2002, a special prosecutor was appointed to investigate decades of allegations against former Chicago Police Cmdr. Jon Burge. The special prosecutor found that Burge and his crew had tortured nearly 200 people during the 1970s to the 1990s. But the statute of limitations had run out on many of the crimes, making them unable to prosecute.</p><p><i>Neither the reporters nor editors who worked on this story — including some represented by the News Guild — have been involved in the litigation described in this article.</i></p>
entries.author: Sophie Sherry

entries.title: Bears transaction tracker: Who's coming, going this offseason
entries.summary: <p>The <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears" target="_blank" >Bears</a> have been busy with a flurry of moves as they try to sharpen their roster — and make prudent salary cap decisions. </p><p>They can start agreeing to deals with free agents Monday, though players can't officially sign their contracts until the start of the league year Wednesday.</p><p>Here's a look at who the Bears have gained and lost: </p><h3>March 12</h3><p>Guard <b>Jordan McFadden</b>, who started in the Bears' playoff loss to the Rams, is returning to the team on a one-year deal. McFadden played 13 games and started twice for the Chargers from 2023-24.</p><h3>March 11</h3><p>Former Browns left tackle <b>Jedrick Wills</b> will join the Bears' starting competition. He'll sign a one-year deal, a source confirmed to the Sun-Times. Wills is a former No. 10 overall pick who started 45 games for the Browns from 2020-22 before being limited by injury the next two years. He sat out last year because of a knee injury. </p><p>Former Bears safety <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears/2026/03/11/bears-star-safety-kevin-byard-to-sign-with-patriots-report" target="_blank" >Kevin Byard</a> is signing a one-year deal with the Patriots to reunite with former Titans coach Mike Vrabel. The Bears said this offseason they'd hope to work out a deal with their team captain and Pro Bowl player but seemed to move on when they gave Coby Bryant a three-year, $40 million deal Monday.</p><p>The Bears agreed to re-sign backup safety <b>Elijah Hicks</b> on a one-year contract Wednesday, a source said. He was a seventh-round pick in general manager Ryan Poles' first draft class in 2022 and has played 61 games (15 starts) for the Bears.</p><p>Former Bears defensive lineman <b>Chris Williams</b> jumped to the Falcons, who now are run by general manager Ian Cunningham, on a one-year, $2.4 million deal, ESPN reported.</p><h3>March 10</h3><p>The Bears added to their defensive line depth Tuesday when they agreed to sign <b>Kentavius Street</b> to a one-year deal, a source confirmed to the Sun-Times. He's played for four teams in his seven years in the league.</p><p>Street spent 2022 playing for the Saints, where Bears defensive coordinator Dennis Allen served as head coach. He posted a career-high 3 ½ sacks that season and has only 10 ½ in his career. Pro Football Focus rated him as the 122<sup>nd</sup>-best run defender among the 224 eligible interior defensive linemen in their system last year. He spent a career-high 104 snaps over tackle in 2022, his year playing for Allen.</p><p>The Bears are signing former Bills defensive back <b>Cam Lewis</b> to a two-year deal, a source confirmed to the Sun-Times on Tuesday.</p><p>Like Coby Bryant, whom the Bears signed a day earlier, Lewis has experience at cornerback, safety and in the slot.</p><p>Lewis, who turns 29 next month, went to college at Buffalo and then spent the first six years of his career with the Bills. He started only 14 games, but 10 of those came over the past two years. Lewis played 38% of the Bills defensive snaps but was a special teams stalwart, playing on 51% of their kicking downs. He’s played at least 51% of his team’s special teams snaps in each of the past four years.</p><p>Former Bears cornerback <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears/2026/03/10/bears-jets-nahshon-wright-agrees-free-agent-deal-interceptions-fumble-receovery-forced-nfl" ><b>Nahshon Wright </b></a>agreed to a one-year, $5.5 million deal with the Jets, per NFL Network. Wright had five interceptions and started all but one game for the Bears last season, one year after the Vikings kept him on their practice squad for most of the season.</p><p> <br>The Bears agreed to a one-year, $5.1 million deal with former Lions wide receiver and return man <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears/2026/03/10/bears-to-sign-former-lions-wide-receiver-returner-kalif-raymond-source" ><b>Kalif Raymond</b></a>. He had 24 catches for 289 yards and a touchdown for the Lions last season.</p><h3>March 9</h3><p>Third-string quarterback <b>Case Keenum</b>, who was a key part of Caleb Williams’ development last year under coach Ben Johnson, is coming back to the Bears, a source confirmed. He’ll make as $5.5 million and as much as $8 million over two years, per ESPN.</p><p>Keenum hasn't thrown a pass since 2023 but has been part of the growth of both Williams and Texans quarterback C.J. Stroud late in his career.</p><p>“He has been an integral part of what we’ve done,” Johnson said in January, days after the Bears’ emotional playoff win against the Packers. “He has been tremendous in terms of Caleb’s growth. That was really the thought process of bringing him in, is: the guy with skins on the wall, he has played in big games himself on a high level.</p><p>"He has won big games, he has lost big games ... it’s something that all these young players can really lean into, particularly in weeks like this where you come off an emotional win and yet you got to turn your sights on the next page pretty quickly."</p><p>After one of the best seasons of his career, former <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears" target="_blank" >Bears</a> linebacker <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears/2026/03/09/former-bears-linebacker-tremaine-edmunds-gets-3-year-36-million-deal-from-giants-report" ><b>Tremaine Edmunds</b></a> got a big contract offer from the Giants. He agreed Monday to a three-year, $36 million deal, sources confirmed to the Sun-Times. It includes $24 million guaranteed.</p><p>Former Bears defensive end <b>Dominique Robinson</b>, a fifth-round pick in 2022, is signing with the Texans on a one-year, $4 milliion deal, ESPN reported.</p><p>The Bears will sign linebacker <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears/2026/03/09/bears-to-sign-playmaking-veteran-devin-bush-jr-as-they-rebuild-linebacker-corps-source" ><b>Devin Bush Jr.</b> </a>from the Browns to a three-year, $30 million deal after the departure of Edmunds, a source said. Bush, a former No. 10 overall pick, had three interceptions and two forced fumbles last season.</p><p>Former Colts defensive tackle <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears/2026/03/09/bears-first-move-in-free-agency-is-deal-with-dt-neville-gallimore-source" ><b>Neville Gallimore</b></a> is joining the Bears on a two-year, $12 million deal when the new league year opens, a source said. He played all 17 games in Indianapolis last season, including eight starts.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center><div class="Enhancement-item"> <div class="ExternalContent-wrapper" > <div class="TweetUrl"> <blockquote class="twitter-tweet"><p lang="en" dir="ltr">Bears Nation let’s do it!!!! Thank you for the opportunity. Let’s work! <a href="https://twitter.com/hashtag/BearDown?src=hash&ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw">#BearDown</a> 🐻</p>— Neville Gallimore (@Path2Greatwork) <a href="https://twitter.com/Path2Greatwork/status/2031059927179972657?ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw">March 9, 2026</a></blockquote> <script async src="https://platform.twitter.com/widgets.js" charset="utf-8"></script> </div> </div> </div></div><p>Linebacker <b>D'Marco Jackson</b> is returning to the Bears on a two-year, $7.5 million deal, a source confirmed. The Bears claimed Jackson on cut day during training camp, citing his experience in coordinator Dennis Allen's system while in New Orleans. He proved to be a solid special teams player and a worthy spot starter. In Week 15, he was named NFC Defensive Player of the Week after logging the first sack and interception of his career in a win against the Browns. </p><p>Receiver <b>Olamide Zaccheaus</b> is leaving the Bears for Atlanta, a source confirmed, agreeing to sign a contract with the Falcons. Zaccheaus spent one year in Chicago after helping the Commanders develop quarterback Jayden Daniels. In Chicago, he struggled with drops — his six were tied for 13<sup>th</sup>-most in the NFL — and by the end of the season was behind DJ Moore, Rome Odunze and Luther Burden on the Bears’ depth chart. He finished with 30 catches for 315 yards. In Atlanta, he’ll be reunited with new general manager Ian Cunningham, who was the Bears’ assistant GM last season. Zaccheaus played in Atlanta from 2019-22, catching passes from new president of football Matt Ryan, the former quarterback.</p><p>With none of their top four safeties under contract for this upcoming season, the Bears needed to move quickly to grab capable players at the position. They found that and more when they <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears/2026/03/09/bears-sign-seahawks-coby-bryant-safety-nfl" target="_blank" >agreed to sign</a> the Seahawks' <b>Coby Bryant</b>. He'll get a three-year, $40 million deal, per ESPN. Bryant, who had four interceptions last year, can play both free safety and strong safety. It's unclear if his additions means Kevin Byard's Bears tenure is over. Both sides had been optimistic about getting a deal done for Byard, who mostly played free safety.</p><h3><b>March 8</b></h3><p>Defensive end <b>Daniel Hardy</b>, who has been a special teams ace, agreed to sign a two-year deal worth as much as $6 million to return to the Bears, a source confirmed to the Sun-Times on Sunday. Hardy played only 5% of the Bears' defensive snaps last year but appeared in a whopping 78% of their kicking plays, totaling 22 tackles.</p><h3><b>March 6</b></h3><p>The day <b>Drew Dalman</b> put in his retirement paperwork, the Bears traded a 2027 fifth-round draft pick to the Patriots for his replacement, center <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears/2026/03/06/bears-trade-garrett-bradbury-patriots-center-2027-fifth-round-pick-nfl" target="_blank" ><b>Garrett Bradbury</b></a>. Bradbury, who started every game for the AFC champions last year, has one year left on a $9.6 million contract he signed last year after spending six years with the Vikings.</p><h3><b>March 5</b></h3><p>Needing to clear cap space and cognizant of their depth at wide receiver, <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/halas-intrigue/2026/03/05/halas-intrigue-podcast-breaking-down-bears-dj-moore-trade-nfl" target="_blank" >the Bears traded</a> <b>DJ Moore</b> and a fifth-round pick to the Bills for a second-round draft pick this year. Moore jad just 682 receiving yards on 50 catches last year, the worst numbers of his career. Had the Bears kept Moore, he would have cost a $24.5 million salary cap hit, the third-highest on the team.</p><p>Eight days after telling linebacker <b>Tremaine Edmunds</b> and his agent that they could seek a trade, the Bears told Edmunds <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears/2026/03/05/bears-release-tremaine-edmunds-linebacker-salary-cap-nfl" target="_blank" >they were cutting him</a>. They saved $15 million in cap space in the process. Edmunds had four interceptions in 13 games last year, the second-most among all NFL linebackers.</p><h3><b>March 3</b></h3><p>Center <b>Drew Dalman</b>, who played every snap and reached the Pro Bowl in his first year with the Bears, told the team he planned to retire after just five years in the NFL. He filed his retirement paperwork three days later.</p><p></p><p></p><p></p><p></p><p></p><bsp-list-loadmore class="PageListH" data-module data-hide-authors="true" data-hide-categories="true" entries.author: Jason Lieser

entries.title: Gobierno federal debate que una mujer de Skokie fuera retenida por ICE durante 30 horas
entries.summary: <p>El Departamento de Seguridad Nacional (DHS) está disputando la historia de Sundas "Sunny" Naqvi, una ciudadana estadounidense de Skokie que <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/la-voz/2026/03/09/funcionarios-de-inmigracion-detienen-a-ciudadana-por-casi-30-horas-en-el-aeropuerto-ohare" target="_blank" >afirmó que ella y sus colegas fueron detenidos</a> durante más de 30 horas por las autoridades federales de inmigración durante el fin de semana.</p><p>La agencia federal afirmó que fue liberada casi una hora después de que fue señalada para una revisión secundaria en el Aeropuerto O'Hare el jueves. La agencia publicó fotos en las redes sociales el miércoles por la noche que supuestamente comprueban eso. Pero Kevin Morrison, comisionado del Condado de Cook y amigo de Naqvi desde la secundaria, dijo al Chicago Sun-Times que las fotos estaban manipuladas.</p><p>"En lugar de publicar una foto claramente manipulada, hago un llamado a DHS para que publique todo el video relevante del Aeropuerto O’Hare de los días y horas en cuestión", dijo Morrison en un comunicado el miércoles por la noche.</p><div class="RichTextSidebarModule Enhancement" data-module data-align-center><a class="AnchorLink" id="module-7d0000" name="module-7d0000"></a> <div class="RichTextModule-items RichTextBody"><h3>La Voz Chicago WhatsApp</h3>Encuentra más noticias en nuestro canal de WhatsApp. <a class="Link" href="https://www.whatsapp.com/channel/0029Vb0XT1XCHDylm3rCs40P" target="_blank" ><b>Síguenos.</b></a></div> </div><p>El domingo, Morrison compartió la versión de Naqvi de los eventos en una conferencia de prensa frente a la instalación de procesamiento del Servicio de Inmigración y Control de Aduanas (ICE) en el suburbio de Broadview. Dijo que Naqvi y cinco de sus colegas fueron detenidos en O’Hare por agentes federales de inmigración debido a su "historial de viajes reciente" tras regresar de Estambul.</p><p>https://bsky.app/profile/morrisonforil.bsky.social/post/3mgh3v4u7m22n</p><p>Naqvi, de 28 años, y los otros fueron llevados a la instalación de Broadview, donde luego fueron transferidos a una instalación en el Condado de Dodge, Wisconsin, el viernes por la noche, según Morrison. Ella fue liberada a eso de las 5 a.m. del sábado después de casi 30 horas.</p><p>La Oficina del Sheriff del Condado de Cook dijo que buscó en la instalación y no encontró nada. La Oficina del Sheriff del Condado de Dodge, Wisconsin, no respondió de inmediato a una solicitud de comentarios.</p><p>Naqvi y sus colegas detenidos rechazaron las solicitudes de entrevista con el Sun-Times en la conferencia de prensa del domingo y no quisieron comentar por teléfono el miércoles. El abogado de Naqvi, Rob Held, tampoco devolvió las llamadas.</p><p>DHS primero desestimó las afirmaciones de Naqvi en un comunicado el lunes, calificando la historia de “descaradamente falsa". La agencia dijo que ella llegó al O’Hare el jueves a las 10:21 a.m., fue referida a una revisión secundaria durante tres minutos, recuperó sus maletas y salió del aeropuerto a las 11:43 a.m. La agencia aseguró que toda la interacción fue captada en video.</p><p>La agencia dijo que se puso en contacto con ella el viernes para pedirle que informara a las personas que estaba bien y confirmó que la Oficina del Sheriff del Condado de Cook había contactado a la instalación de procesamiento de ICE en Broadview acerca de ella.</p><p>La agencia federal también señaló el historial delictivo de Naqvi. Naqvi se declaró culpable en 2022 de hacer un reporte de policía falso alegando agresión sexual y cumplió dos años de libertad condicional, según los registros del condado. El caso fue luego desestimado como parte de un acuerdo de enjuiciamiento diferido.</p><p>El martes, la agencia dijo: “La Señora Naqvi no fue detenida ni transferida a ICE para su detención”.</p><p>A última hora del miércoles, el DHS publicó capturas de pantalla en las redes sociales que supuestamente muestran a Naqvi saliendo de la revisión secundaria.</p><p>Morrison dijo que contenían una "foto claramente manipulada" y pidió a la agencia que publicara todos sus videos.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center><div class="Enhancement-item"> <div class="ExternalContent-wrapper" > <div class="TweetUrl"> <blockquote class="twitter-tweet"><p lang="en" dir="ltr">HERE ARE THE RECEIPTS:<br><br>As we said Sunny Naqvi entered the CBP area at 10:21 am.<br><br>Surveillance footage from O’Hare CLEARLY shows her entering secondary inspection at 10:46 a.m., and leaving secondary to the public area at 11:42 a.m.<br><br>Her claims of spending 43 hours in DHS custody… <a href="https://t.co/GkqWBLS6sn">https://t.co/GkqWBLS6sn</a> <a href="https://t.co/SWOJmMulcy">pic.twitter.com/SWOJmMulcy</a></p>— Homeland Security (@DHSgov) <a href="https://twitter.com/DHSgov/status/2031879248068194404?ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw">March 11, 2026</a></blockquote> <script async src="https://platform.twitter.com/widgets.js" charset="utf-8"></script> </div> </div> </div></div><p>Morrison no está solo en cuestionar la credibilidad de la agencia federal.</p><p>Un juez federal encontró que el excomandante de la Patrulla Fronteriza, Gregory Bovino, <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/la-voz/2025/12/15/greg-bovino-es-la-estrella-del-show-de-deportacion-de-trump-investigamos-su-pasado" target="_blank" >mintió en una declaración judicial</a> en noviembre sobre su propio uso de gas lacrimógeno en una confrontación con manifestantes en el vecindario de La Villita. Y 17 de 32 demandados en casos federales relacionados con el Operativo Midway Blitz han sido desestimados, tres de los cuales se desestimaron después de que los grandes jurados se negaron a presentar cargos.</p><p>Poco después de que los agentes le dispararon y mataron a Silverio Villegas González el pasado septiembre en el suburbio de Franklin Park, DHS afirmó que un agente estaba “gravemente herido” tras ser supuestamente arrastrado por el vehículo de González. Pero videos de cámaras corporales de la policía mostraron más tarde que el agente dijo que estaba <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/la-voz/2025/09/23/video-muestra-que-agente-de-ice-dijo-que-sus-heridas-no-eran-graves-en-tiroteo-de-silverio-villegas-gonzalez" target="_blank" >“sólo un poco alterado”</a>.</p><p>Las grabaciones liberadas en febrero también <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/la-voz/2026/02/11/agentes-dijeron-es-hora-de-ser-agresivos-en-video-que-contradice-las-afirmaciones-contra-marimar-martinez" target="_blank" >contradicen la versión de la agencia</a> sobre el momento en que uno de sus agentes le disparó a Marimar Martínez en el vecindario de Brighton Park el otoño pasado. DHS luego dijo que “defiende nuestros comunicados de prensa y declaraciones. Los hechos de lo que sucedió no cambiaron”.</p><p><i>Traducido con una herramienta de inteligencia artificial (AI) y editado por </i><a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/la-voz" target="_blank" ><i>La Voz Chicago</i></a></p>
entries.author: Mohammad Samra

entries.title: Sweet-shooting Goreville knocks off Lawrenceville in 1A semis
entries.summary: <p>CHAMPAIGN—Goreville can shoot. The Blackcats arrived at the state finals as one of the most prolific three-point threats in state history. They made 326 threes this season, good enough for a top 20 spot on the all-time list.</p><p>But many great shooting high school teams have found State Farm Center a difficult place adjust to, especially in the first half of the first game. And especially if you are used to playing in small Class 1A gyms.</p><p>“Man, it is a whole lot different playing in this big of a place,” Goreville senior Garrett Church said. “There is no backdrop, so you really have to focus on the rim and not what is behind it. Once I got into the flow of the game it definitely helped me.”</p><p>Church made eight threes in the Blackcats’ supersectional win, and he hit three in the 55-47 win against Lawrenceville in the Class 1A semifinals on <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/high-school-basketball/scores/2026/03/09/ihsa-boys-basketball-state-finals-scores-illinois" >Thursday</a>. The Blackcats were 6-for-14 from three-point range.</p><p>Goreville (35-4) will face <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/high-school-basketball/2026/03/12/marshall-basketball-chicago-ihsa-state-hoop-dreams" >Marshall</a> in the championship game on Saturday. </p><p>“Our confidence is through the roof,” Blackcats junior Nick King said. “We all trust each other, off the court as well. We trust each other with all our hearts.”</p><p>King led Goreville with 20 points and five rebounds. Church scored nine and Tommy King and Braden Compton each added eight points.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure class="Figure"><a like [Garrett Church], are really smart. When you mix the quickness, the smarts, the guts and the heart it all comes together.”</p><p>Goreville held Lawrenceville (34-3) to just five points in the third quarter and led 36-24 heading into the fourth.</p><p>“They play really good man-to-man defense, and they make you feel uncomfortable with how hard they play and how they get up in you,” Lawrenceville coach Tad Shick said.</p><p>Jayden Goff led Lawrenceville with 16 points and six rebounds and Noah Wilson scored 11.</p><p>“We were a little rattled at the beginning,” Shick said. “That’s expected coming up here for the first time. I really liked how we played late in the fourth quarter. We showed a lot of heart. The game was out of hand but these guys didn’t quit playing.”</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-750000" name="html-embed-module-750000"></a><iframe title="No Shot Clock, Ep. 205: Previewing the 2026 IHSA state finals" allowtransparency="true" height="150" width="100%" style="border: none; min-width: min(100%, 430px);height:150px;" scrolling="no" data-name="pb-iframe-player" src="https://www.podbean.com/player-v2/?i=my6cv-1a6a650-pb&from=pb6admin&share=1&download=1&rtl=0&fonts=Arial&skin=1&font-color=auto&logo_link=episode_page&btn-skin=c73a3a" loading="lazy"></iframe> </div> </div><p></p>

entries.title: Are tornadoes surging in Illinois? What you need to know
entries.summary: <p>A couple in Northwest Indiana <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/weather/2026/03/11/2-killed-after-storms-bring-at-least-4-twisters-possible-record-setting-hail-in-illinois-and-indiana" >is dead after a tornado</a> leveled their home this week.</p><p>Potentially record-sized hail pummeled Kankakee <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/chicago-suburbs" >south of Chicago</a>.</p><p>Here’s what you need to know about this extreme <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/weather" >weather</a> and how to keep yourself safe.</p><p><b>Are more tornadoes hitting Illinois and Chicago than in the past?</b></p><p>It's difficult to find year-over-year records but Illinois last year had more than 140 tornadoes, putting it neck and neck with Texas for the most of any U.S. state. And numbers across the country have been rising over the past 50 years.</p><p>Tornadoes have ripped through Illinois for more than a century. The deadliest tornado in U.S. history passed through Southern Illinois, Missouri and Indiana in 1925, killing almost 700 people.</p><p><b>Can tornadoes touch down in Chicago?</b></p><p>Yes, and they have. The Chicago area has recorded devastating tornadoes, including one that tore through Plainfield and nearby communities in 1990, killing 29, injuring hundreds and causing $165 million in damage.</p><p>While the most lethal and damaging twisters have taken place outside the city, they can hit anywhere. Lake Michigan can even make conditions for a potential storm worse in the summer.</p><p><b>How can I keep my family and I safe from such severe storms and tornadoes, in particular?</b></p><p>Stay informed if there are severe weather warnings in your area.</p><p>“Everybody should have a severe weather plan. Don’t be in a car and don’t be in a mobile home,” said Victor Gensini, director at the Center for Interdisciplinary Research on Convective Storms at Northern Illinois University.</p><p>Don’t be lulled into thinking that a twister can’t hit a city like Chicago, he said. It’s important to consider, he adds, that they’re relatively uncommon events compared with thunderstorms that can lead to widespread flooding.</p><p>“These are very rare events,” Gensini said. “Flooding is way, way more common.”</p><p><b>Are these storms and tornadoes caused by climate change?</b></p><p>Extreme heat is linked to climate change and strong thunderstorms are believed to be exacerbated by global warming. But the causes of tornadoes are more complex, according to Illinois State Climatologist Trent Ford.</p><p>“There remains uncertainty of the extent to which climate change has caused these recent trends,” Ford wrote in a recent report. “However, it is thought the warmer and more humid climate in Illinois has had at least some effect on the increasing frequency in severe storm environments.”</p><p>Gensini, who witnessed the devastating tornado in Granville and Utica as a high school senior in 2004, agrees. But he said the trends are still pointing toward more severe weather.</p><p>“Regardless of climate change, the reality is that we're going to have more and more and more of these disasters,” Gensini said.</p><p><b>Is Illinois the new Tornado Alley?</b></p><p>The phrase is a “media colloquialism” coined in the early 1950s and is in no way an official designation, Gensini said.</p><p>“If I ask 100 of my meteorology friends to define, they’d each have a different answer,” he added.</p><p>According to some definitions, Illinois has actually been considered part of Tornado Alley for some time. Initially, the nickname applied to southwestern states, including Kansas, Oklahoma and Texas that were ravaged by twisters in the 20th Century.</p><p>But weather patterns over the past half century have pointed to a rising number of tornadoes east of the Mississippi River, including in Michigan.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure entries.title: 22 things to do in Chicago March 12-18
entries.summary: <h3>Theater</h3><ul class="rte2-style-ul" id="rte-0b71b570-1c0d-11f1-ae62-6db9e841aaa4"><li>Giacomo Puccini’s heartbreaking opera, <b>“Madama Butterfly,”</b> the story of a faithful but doomed geisha (soprano Karah Son) in love with an American Navy lieutenant (tenor Evan LeRoy Johnson), is presented in Matthew Ozawa’s new reinterpretation, which encourages a deeper understanding of the story’s legacy and impact today. Domingo Hindoyan conducts. From March 14-April 12 at Lyric Opera, 20 N. Wacker Drive. Tickets: $52+. Visit <a class="Link" href="http://lyricopera.org" target="_blank" ><u>lyricopera.org</u></a>.</li></ul><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure class="Figure"><a class="AnchorLink" id="image-bb0000" name="image-bb0000"></a> <picture data-crop="medium"> <source type="image/webp" width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/179cf9a/2147483647/strip/true/crop/2048x1149+0+120/resize/490x275!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F43%2F3d%2F097fdeb24bf481a1d848f9ca0b28%2Ffully-committed-actor-mike-newquist-photo-by-derek-bertelsen.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/4b7b4d3/2147483647/strip/true/crop/2048x1149+0+120/resize/980x550!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F43%2F3d%2F097fdeb24bf481a1d848f9ca0b28%2Ffully-committed-actor-mike-newquist-photo-by-derek-bertelsen.jpg 2x" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <source width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/3a035a5/2147483647/strip/true/crop/2048x1149+0+120/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F43%2F3d%2F097fdeb24bf481a1d848f9ca0b28%2Ffully-committed-actor-mike-newquist-photo-by-derek-bertelsen.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <img class="Image" alt="Fully Committed - Visit <a class="Link" href="http://oldtownschool.org" target="_blank" ><u>oldtownschool.org</u></a>.</li><li>The finals of the poetry festival <b>Young Chicago Authors’ Rooted and Radical </b>features finalists from the Chicagoland area performing their original pieces. class="Figure-content"><figcaption class="Figure-caption"><p>Chicago Plumbers Local 130 dye the Chicago River green last year.</p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Pat Nabong/Sun-Times file</p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p></p><ul class="rte2-style-ul" id="rte-0b71dc89-1c0d-11f1-ae62-6db9e841aaa4"><li>This weekend it’s all about the Irish beginning with the annual dyeing of the Chicago River (10 a.m. March 14) followed by two traditional parades. The 71th annual Chicago <b>St. Patrick’s Day Parade, </b>one of the largest in the country, begins at noon March 14 and lasts for about three hours on Columbus Drive between Balbo Drive and Monroe Drive (<a class="Link" href="http://chicagostpatricksdayparade.org" target="_blank" ><u>chicagostpatricksdayparade.org</u></a>). The 48th annual <b>South Side Irish Parade </b>steps off at noon March 15 and lasts for about two hours on Western Avenue from 103rd Street to 115th Street in the Beverly neighborhood (<a class="Link" href="http://southsideirishparade.org" target="_blank" ><u>southsideirishparade.org</u></a>).</li><li>One of the best area celebrations takes place at the <b>Irish American Heritage Center</b>, 4626 N. Knox Ave. From 12:30-11 p.m. March 14 find a full lineup of Irish music, dance, food, children’s activities and more ($15-$30). On the day itself, from 2-10 p.m. March 17 enjoy music by Peter Brady and The Chancey Brothers ($15). Visit <a class="Link" href="http://irish-american.org" target="_blank" ><u>irish-american.org</u></a>.</li><li>Old St. Pat’s family-friendly <b>Shamrock’n </b>features Irish pub food, music, raffle and activities for children. From 11 a.m.-5 p.m. March 14 at 625 W. Adams St. Tickets: $35+, children 6-10 $10, free for 5 and under. Visit <a class="Link" href="http://oldstpats.org/shamrockn.html" target="_blank" ><u>oldstpats.org/shamrockn.html</u></a>.</li><li><b>Shamrockin’ at the Pier</b> is a pub crawl through restaurant/bars and a docked cruise ship that includes drink specials, live music, games and more. From 8 a.m.-7 p.m. March 14 at Navy Pier, 600 E. Grand Ave. Tickets: $20+. Visit <a class="Link" href="http://navypier.org" target="_blank" ><u>navypier.org</u></a>.</li></ul>
entries.author: Mary Houlihan

entries.title: Where to find Chicago-area food pantries
entries.summary: <p>Below is a list of some pantries serving residents, regardless of immigration status, across the Chicago area.</p><p><b>Pantries or organizations with multiple locations</b><br></p><ul class="rte2-style-ul" id="rte-a68451b0-fb8f-11f0-9843-17be6eed4696"><li><a class="Link" href="https://www.chicagosfoodbank.org/find-food-2/#find-food" target="_blank" ><u>The Greater Chicago Food Depository</u></a></li><li><a class="Link" href="http://www.solvehungertoday.org/getgroceries" target="_blank" >Northern Illinois Food Bank local food pantry search tool</a></li><li><a class="Link" href="https://www.theunityincommunity.org/local-food-pantries" target="_blank" ><u>The Unity in Community Foundation</u></a></li></ul><p><b>North</b><br></p><ul class="rte2-style-ul" id="rte-a6849fd2-fb8f-11f0-9843-17be6eed4696"><li><a class="Link" href="https://www.commonpantry.org/" target="_blank" ><u>Common Pantry</u></a>, 3908 N. Lincoln Ave., 773-327-0553 </li><li><a class="Link" href="https://friendshipcenterchicago.org/" target="_blank" ><u>The Friendship Center</u></a>, 2711 W. Lawrence Ave., 773-907-6388 </li><li><a class="Link" href="https://www.irvingparkfoodpantry.org/" target="_blank" ><u>The Irving Park Community Food Pantry</u></a>, 4256 N. Ridgeway Ave., 773-283-6296 </li><li><a class="Link" href="https://msvchicago.org/the-harvest-hub/" target="_blank" ><u>Marillac St. Vincent Harvest Hub Food Pantry</u></a>, 2145 N. Halsted, 312-278-4130</li><li><a class="Link" href="https://www.nourishinghopechi.org/" target="_blank" ><u>Nourishing Hope’s Sheridan Market</u></a>, 3945 N. Sheridan Road, 773-525-1777 </li><li><a class="Link" href="https://onwardhouse.org/support/family-support-services/" target="_blank" ><u>Onward Neighborhood House</u></a>, 5433 W. Diversey Ave., 773-622-3215</li><li><a class="Link" href="https://ccolife.org/" target="_blank" ><u>Cornerstone Community Outreach</u></a>, 4628 N. Clifton Ave., 773-271-8163 ext. 0</li><li><a class="Link" href="https://careforreal.org/get-help/food-pantry-information/" target="_blank" >Care for Real</a>, 1545 W. Morse Ave., 773-769-6182 </li><li><a class="Link" href="https://www.icnarelief.org/chicago/food-pantry/" target="_blank" >ICNA Relief</a>, 2809 W. Devon Ave., 844-414-4862</li></ul><p><b>South</b><br></p><ul class="rte2-style-ul" id="rte-a6853c10-fb8f-11f0-9843-17be6eed4696"><li><a class="Link" href="https://www.facebook.com/ABlessingHand/" target="_blank" ><u>A Blessing Hand</u></a>, 5815 S Homan Ave., (312) 597-5483</li><li><a class="Link" href="https://attcomcharities.org/food-pantry-2/" target="_blank" ><u>All Things Through Christ</u></a>, 6600 S. Hermitage Ave., 708-654-7826</li><li><a class="Link" href="https://www.bravespacealliance.org/programs-services-resources-food" target="_blank" ><u>Brave Space Alliance,</u></a>1515 E. 52nd Place, 872-333-5199</li><li><a class="Link" href="https://www.coppinchicago.org/" target="_blank" ><u>Coppin Community Center</u></a>, 5627 S Michigan Ave., (773) 667-5881</li><li><a class="Link" href="https://www.egifoundation.org/" target="_blank" ><u>Edward G. Irvin Foundation’s food pantry</u></a>, 500 E. 67th St., (312) 852-6923</li><li><a class="Link" href="https://www.pilsenfoodpantry.com/" target="_blank" ><u>Pilsen Food Pantry</u></a>, 2124 S. Ashland Ave., 773-812-3150 </li><li><a class="Link" href="https://saintsabina.org/outreach/st-sabina-social-services/social-services-food-pantry.html" target="_blank" ><u>St. Sabina Church’s food pantry</u></a>, 1120 W. 79th St., 773-483-4300 </li><li><a class="Link" href="https://www.swcollective.org/." target="_blank" ><u>The Southwest Collective</u>, </a>5525 S. Pulaski Road, 708-740-8914 </li><li><a class="Link" href="https://www.salvationarmyusa.org/il/chicago/west-69th-street-corps/contact-us/" target="_blank" ><u>The Salvation Army - Adele and Robert Stern Red Shield Center</u></a>, 945 W. 69th St., 773-358-3200 </li></ul><p><b>WEST</b><br></p><ul class="rte2-style-ul" id="rte-a6858a32-fb8f-11f0-9843-17be6eed4696"><li><a class="Link" href="https://www.chipublib.org/locations/42/" target="_blank" >Legler Regional Library</a>, 115 S. Pulaski Rd., (312) 746-7730</li><li><a class="Link" href="https://www.anb.tips/" target="_blank" ><u>Above and Beyond Food Pantry</u></a>, 817 S. Pulaski Road, 773-823-1660 </li><li><a class="Link" href="https://msvchicago.org/the-harvest-hub/" target="_blank" ><u>Marillac St. Vincent Harvest Hub Food Pantry</u></a>, 2859 W. Jackson, 773-584-4539</li><li><a class="Link" href="https://www.nourishinghopechi.org/" target="_blank" ><u>Nourishing Hope’s El Mercadito</u></a>, 3533 W. North Ave., 773-525-1777</li></ul><p><b>Suburbs</b><br></p><ul class="rte2-style-ul" id="rte-a685b141-fb8f-11f0-9843-17be6eed4696"><li><a class="Link" href="https://www.gobeyondhunger.org/" target="_blank" >Beyond Hunger</a>, 848 Lake Street, Oak Park, 708-386-1324</li><li><a class="Link" href="https://www.morainetownship.org/food-pantry" target="_blank" ><u>Moraine Township Food Pantry</u></a>, 800 Central Ave., Highland Park, 847-432-3240  </li><li><a class="Link" href="https://nilestownshipgov.com/162/Food-Pantry" target="_blank" ><u>Niles Township Food Pantry</u></a>, 8341 Lockwood Ave., Skokie, 847-983-0073 </li><li><a class="Link" href="https://togetherwecope.org/" target="_blank" ><u>Together We Cope</u></a>, 17010 S. Oak Park Ave., Tinley Park, 708-633-5040 </li><li><a class="Link" href="https://schaumburgtownship.org/services/food-pantry/" target="_blank" ><u>Township of Schaumburg Food Pantry</u></a>, 1 Illinois Blvd., Hoffman Estates, 847-285-4503</li><li><a class="Link" href="https://wscpantry.org/" target="_blank" ><u>West Suburban Community Pantry,</u></a> 6809 Hobson Valley Drive, Suite 118, Woodridge, 630-512-9921 </li></ul><p><i>Most food pantries generally do not require people to provide an identification card, though some pantries might ask to see one depending on the services being provided. Some pantries also ask for a person’s ZIP code. </i></p>

entries.title: Trump says Iranian soccer team playing in World Cup would not be 'appropriate'
entries.summary: <p>WASHINGTON — President Donald Trump said he did not think it would be "appropriate" for the Iranian soccer team to attend this year's World Cup, co-hosted by the United States, and cited safety concerns as a reason on Thursday while the countries remained embroiled in a war.</p><p>"The Iran National Soccer Team is welcome to The World Cup," Trump wrote on his social media site, "but I really don't believe it is appropriate that they be there, for their own life and safety."</p><p>Iranian leaders said earlier this week that it's "not possible" for the country to participate in the World Cup.</p><p>Trump's message appears to depart somewhat from what the Republican president relayed Tuesday at the White House to FIFA President Gianni Infantino, who later publicly said that Trump assured him the Iranian players and coaches would be welcome.</p><p>A White House official, who insisted on anonymity to discuss private conversations, had confirmed Trump's message to Infantino about Iran's participation.</p><p>On Thursday, the White House did not immediately clarify what Trump meant by "their own life and safety," such as whether he anticipated threats against them while in the United States after U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran that began Feb. 28.</p><p>Iran, one of 48 teams in the tournament, is scheduled to play in Inglewood, California, against New Zealand on June 15 and Belgium on June 21 before finishing group play in Seattle against Egypt on June 26. The U.S. is hosting the tournament with Canada and Mexico from June 11 to July 19.</p><p>Iran's soccer federation has planned to take the team in June to a tournament base camp in Arizona, at the Kino Sports Complex in Tucson.</p><p>Since June, Iran has been subject to a travel ban into the U.S. as part of the Trump administration's immigration crackdown. But athletes and coaches from the target nations are exempt, which means the Iranian team would be allowed to enter the U.S.</p><p>But there are also likely fears from Iranian soccer players about playing in a tournament abroad where they could be feted by an anti-regime diaspora while their families face threats back home.</p><p>The Iranian women's soccer team, which arrived in Australia to play at its Asian Cup tournament before the U.S. and Israeli bombing attacks on Iran started, did not sing the Iranian national anthem before its first game. That was widely interpreted as a gesture of protest or an act of mourning. Several members of the team stayed in Australia on humanitarian visas afterward.</p><p>At the 2022 men's World Cup, played in Qatar, the Iranian team did not sing the anthem before a game against England and did not celebrate the two goals scored in a 6-2 loss. At that time, Iran was in turmoil several weeks after the death in police custody of 22-year-old Mahsa Amini, who had been detained for allegedly violating a strict Islamic dress code.</p><p>FIFA's own evaluation was "low risk" for World Cup safety and security plans proposed by the U.S., Canada and Mexico soccer federations, which are guaranteed by their governments. Trump has often taken credit for "winning" the World Cup hosting rights in 2018, when the three neighbors easily beat Morocco in a vote by FIFA member federations.</p><p>"All parties have experience of hosting major sports events on a regular basis and established arrangements are in place for managing security and safety at stadiums and for high-profile individuals," FIFA's in-house inspection team wrote eight years ago.</p><p>Iranian athletes who previously defied the Islamic regime have left the country to continue their careers.</p><p>Iran's first female athlete to win an Olympic medal, Kimia Alizadeh, a bronze medalist at the 2016 Rio de Janeiro Games in taekwondo, criticized wearing the mandatory hijab headscarf. She competed for the Olympic refugee team at Tokyo in 2021 and for Bulgaria at the 2024 Paris Olympics.</p><p>Judoka Saeid Mollaei went into hiding in Germany after a dispute with Iranian team officials at the 2019 world championships. Mollaei, the defending champion, said he was ordered to lose a bout to avoid a potential gold medal match against an Israeli opponent. He got Mongolian citizenship and took silver at the 2020 Tokyo Olympics.<br></p>
entries.author: AP

entries.title: Marshall returns to Champaign for the first time since Hoop Dreams and wins a 1A semifinal
entries.summary: <p>CHAMPAIGN — It’s been 35 years since Arthur Agee’s Marshall team, documented in the movie "Hoop Dreams," lost to Peoria Manual in the state semifinals.</p><p>On <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/high-school-basketball/scores/2026/03/09/ihsa-boys-basketball-state-finals-scores-illinois" >Thursday</a>, The Commandos returned to Champaign for the first time since that famously chronicled 1991 season.</p><p>And this time, Marshall got the win. The Commandos beat Eastland 66-38 in the Class 1A semifinals at State Farm Center. Marshall will face Goreville in the state championship game on Saturday.</p><p>“It felt great out there,” Commandos senior Quinton Gibson said. “At first I was a little shaky with the bigger court, but after we got going the chemistry got better and we had a good time with all that space.”</p><p>Gibson led Marshall (23-10) with 20 points and six rebounds.</p><p>“Quinton played an excellent game defensively as well,” Commandos coach Darrin Laye said. “We held their best player to seven points. It is a testament to his high energy.”</p><p>Parker Krogman, Eastland’s best player, picked up two fouls in the first five minutes and finished with seven points.</p><p>“Obviously I had a guy in my shorts the whole game,” Krogman said. “Not that it hasn’t happened [before]. But I don’t think I handled that as well as I should have.”</p><p>Eastland scored just eight points in the first half and shot 13 of 45 in the game.</p><p>“It started with the pressure that comes from the point guard,” Laye said. “The last couple of games [junior Edward Humes] has been tremendous with turning other teams point guards and forcing them to be a little helter skelter. He’s been amazing for us hawking the basketball.”</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure class="Figure"><a class="AnchorLink" class="Figure-content"><figcaption class="Figure-caption"><p>Marshall’s Edward Humes, left, and Quinton Gibson celebrate as the Commandos close out a 66-38 victory over Eastland to advance to the Class 1A state championship game.</p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Allen Cunningham/For the Sun-Times</p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p>Marshall assisted on 18 of its 29 baskets. Junior guard Rayvon Miles contributed 14 points, eight rebounds, seven assists and six steals.</p><p>The heightened atmosphere and large court is often difficult for high school teams to acclimate to in the first quarter. Myles’ passing provided easy buckets for Gibson in the first few minutes to help the Commandos off to a strong start.</p><p>“They are finally understanding what we mean when we talk about discipline and playing for each other,” Laye said. “It is all about growth now. They’ve been hearing me say it all year. When we create for each other it is so much easier.”</p><p>Sophomore guard DJ Laye Jr. added eight points and four assists for Marshall. Amari Kennedy (eight points) and Semere Williams (five points) came off the bench and scored in the second quarter to help the Commandos establish a 20-3 lead.</p><p>Marshall finished third in Class 3A in 2010 and won the Class 3A state title in 2008. Both of those tournaments were held in Peoria. The Commandos also won titles in 1958 and 1960.</p><p>“[Winning on Saturday] would be huge for the program,” Darrin Laye said. “This is a legendary program and we get so much support. There are a lot of alumni around. [Agee] comes back. There is a buzz around the building. We set out to bring that tradition back to Marshall.”</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-750000" name="html-embed-module-750000"></a><iframe title="No Shot Clock, Ep. 205: Previewing the 2026 IHSA state finals" allowtransparency="true" height="150" width="100%" style="border: none; min-width: min(100%, 430px);height:150px;" scrolling="no" data-name="pb-iframe-player" src="https://www.podbean.com/player-v2/?i=my6cv-1a6a650-pb&from=pb6admin&share=1&download=1&rtl=0&fonts=Arial&skin=1&font-color=auto&logo_link=episode_page&btn-skin=c73a3a" loading="lazy"></iframe> </div> </div><p></p>

entries.title: CTA Yellow Line suspended due to medical emergency in Skokie
entries.summary: <p>CTA Yellow Line service has been suspended due to a medical emergency on the tracks near Skokie.</p><p>Skokie police and fire departments responded to the emergency around 12:40 p.m. Thursday near the Skokie station at 5005 Dempster St., according to a CTA spokesperson.</p><p>The condition of the person and circumstances of the emergency were not immediately released.</p><p>Shuttle buses are transporting CTA passengers between Yellow Line stations while service is suspended.</p><p>Service should resume shortly, the CTA spokesperson said.</p><p><i>Check back for updates</i></p>
entries.author: Sun-Times Wire

entries.title: IHSA boys basketball state finals schedule and scores
entries.summary: <p>The complete schedule and all the scores from the 2026 IHSA state finals at State Farm Center in Champaign.</p><h3><b>Thursday, March 12</b></h3><p><b>Class 1A Semifinals</b></p><p><a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/high-school-basketball/2026/03/12/goreville-basketball-lawrenceville-ihsa-state-finals" >Goreville 55, Lawrenceville 47 </a></p><p><a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/high-school-basketball/2026/03/12/marshall-basketball-chicago-ihsa-state-hoop-dreams" >Marshall 66, Eastland 38</a></p><p><b>Class 2A Semifinals</b></p><p>Tolono Unity vs. Peoria Manual, 2:30 p.m.</p><p>Quincy Notre Dame vs. Farragut, 4:15 p.m.</p><p><b>Third Place</b></p><p>1A: Lawrenceville vs. Eastland, 7 p.m.</p><p>Class 2A, 8:45 p.m.</p><h3>Friday, March 13</h3><p><b>Class 3A Semifinals</b></p><p>Kaneland vs. Deerfield, 10 a.m.</p><p>East St. Louis vs. Leo, 11:45 a.m.</p><p><b>Class 4A Semifinals</b></p><p>Marist vs. York, 2:30 p.m.</p><p>Benet vs. DePaul Prep, 4:15 p.m.</p><p><b>Third Place</b></p><p>Class 3A, 7 p.m.</p><p>Class 4A, 8:45 p.m.</p><h3>Saturday, March 14</h3><p><b>State Championships</b></p><p>1A: Goreville vs. Marshall, 10 a.m.</p><p>Class 2A, noon</p><p>Class 3A, 4 p.m.</p><p>Class 4A, 6 p.m.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-750000" name="html-embed-module-750000"></a><iframe title="No Shot Clock, Ep. 205: Previewing the 2026 IHSA state finals" allowtransparency="true" height="150" width="100%" style="border: none; min-width: min(100%, 430px);height:150px;" scrolling="no" data-name="pb-iframe-player" src="https://www.podbean.com/player-v2/?i=my6cv-1a6a650-pb&from=pb6admin&share=1&download=1&rtl=0&fonts=Arial&skin=1&font-color=auto&logo_link=episode_page&btn-skin=c73a3a" loading="lazy"></iframe> </div> </div><p></p>
